content.body: Camille (European Commission) just made some big declaration in the Annex K meeting about what is needed for presumption of conformity but I didn't really catch it

I guess the part talking about the requirements in K 1.1 ii) for non-ACM things would be per-vertical, rather than the specified in Annex K. So we don't have to worry about the "two years" part -- we can choose that
however, it sounds like the vertical does need to publish the catalog which we add to ACM for acceptable crypto algorithms. Marvin Petzolt (NordSecurity) is this your understanding?
content.formatted_body: however, it sounds like the vertical does need to publish the catalog which we add to ACM for acceptable crypto algorithms. Marvin Petzolt (NordSecurity) is this your understanding?

content.body: From what I got now, yes, the verticals are expected by EC to publish a dedicated  cryptographic algorithm catalogue.
but Annex K seems to have references to industry catalogs. Do those need to be referenced by the vertical to be usable?
also I'm confused why there are assessment steps related to evaluating the algorithm. Don't we do all this evaluation in the process of creating the standard instead?

No Annex K does not have references to industry catalogs, it just mentions that industry catalogs need to be defined by the verticals. 

> also I'm confused why there are assessment steps related to evaluating the algorithm. Don't we do all this evaluation in the process of creating the standard instead?
Yes, this part I am also not getting yet. Is Annex K intended for the verticals or the manufacturers who are adopting the standard.
